name: ChatALL
tagline: Chat with many AI bots at once and compare their answers side by side
seoTitle: ChatALL: Query Many AI Chatbots at Once
metaDescription: ChatALL is a free, open-source desktop app that sends one prompt to ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini and dozens more AI bots at once so you can compare answers.
overview: ChatALL is a free, open-source desktop app that lets you send a single prompt to many AI chatbots at the same time. Instead of opening each service one by one, you type your question once and watch the answers come back together, so you can spot the best result fast.

It runs as a local client on Windows, macOS, and Linux. ChatALL is not a proxy: it connects to each bot using your own accounts or API keys, and all chat history, settings, and login data stay on your own computer.
The app supports a wide range of bots, including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, Poe, Groq Cloud, and many open models, with both web-access and API connections depending on the service.
features: Send one prompt to several AI bots concurrently and compare their answers together
Connect to dozens of bots, including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, Groq, and many open models, via web access or API
Quick-prompt mode lets you send the next prompt without waiting for the previous reply to finish
Highlight the responses you like and delete the weak ones to keep the best results
Enable or disable any bot at any time and switch between one-, two-, or three-column views
Keeps all chat history and settings locally, with dark mode, multi-chat, proxy settings, and many languages

gettingStarted.intro: ChatALL ships as a ready-to-run desktop app, so most users just download an installer. If you want to run it from source, you need Node.js and npm.

steps.heading: Download and install the app
steps.body: Grab the installer for your platform from the GitHub releases page: a *-win.exe on Windows, a *-mac-arm64.dmg or *-mac-x64.dmg on macOS, or a .deb / .AppImage on Linux. Then open it and follow the setup.

steps.heading: Install on macOS with Homebrew
steps.body: If you use Homebrew on macOS, you can install ChatALL with a single command instead of downloading the disk image.

steps.heading: Add your accounts or API keys
steps.body: ChatALL is a client, not a proxy, so you must have working accounts or API tokens for the bots you want to use, plus a reliable network connection. Log in or paste your keys through the in-app settings, then enable the bots you want.

steps.heading: Run from source (developers)
steps.body: To work on ChatALL itself, clone the repository, install the dependencies, and start the Electron dev server.

useCases: Power users who want to find the best answer by comparing several AI bots on the same prompt at once
Researchers comparing the strengths and weaknesses of different language models across tasks
Developers debugging prompts and quickly testing which foundation model performs best for their app
